title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
At the Blaux Han Hotel, Hercule Poirot and his companion discuss the disappearance of Miss Fay. Luca Lazari, a hotel representative, attempts to distract Poirot with descriptions of the hotel's luxury, but Poirot remains focused on three recent murders. The victims—Mrs. Harriet Sipper, Miss Edith Gransbury, and Mr. Richard Nigges—were guests who checked into the hotel separately on Tuesday and were found dead in their locked rooms the following day.

The victims were killed alone in their respective rooms, and their room keys are missing, leading Poirot to suspect the killer took them along with several cufflinks. The cause of death appears to be cyanide poisoning, administered through tea consumed by Sipper and Gransbury, and sherry consumed by Nigges. Forensic examinations place the time of death between 7:15 PM and 8:20 PM.

Lazari claims that at 7:15 PM, staff saw all three victims when room service food was delivered. Poirot finds the coincidence of three separate guests checking in on the same day, dying on the same day, and requesting room service at the exact same time to be impossible. To investigate these discrepancies, Poirot demands that Lazari gather all hotel staff in an empty room for questioning. Meanwhile, Poirot and his companion proceed to the victims' rooms to conduct a thorough search.
